resources.string: • A regular polygon has equal sides and angles. \n • Its centre is intersection of inscribed and circumscribed circles. \n • Apothem = perpendicular to side; radius = centre to vertex.

• Interior angle α=(180°*(n−2))/n. 
 • Inscribed and circumscribed circles share the same centre. 
 • Area = ½ × perimeter × apothem = ½ × n × R² × sin(360°/n). 
 • Side length s=2R*sin(180/n).
• Pi (π) ≈ 3.1416, ratio of circumference to diameter.
• Circumference ratios = radius or diameter ratios. \n • C=πD=2πR, where D->diameter of the circle, R->circle radius, C->circle length. \n • Arc length l=(πR/180°)*n° for central angle n°.
• Sector: The portion of a circle bounded by two radii and one of the arcs they intercept. \n • Segment: The region of a circle bounded by a chord and the arc subtended by that chord.

• Circle Area Formula: Computed by the formula S=πR^2. \n • Area of a Sector: Calculated as S=[(πR^2)/360∘]*α, where α is the degree measure of the central angle. \n • Area of a Segment: Computed by S=[(πR^2)/360∘]*α±SΔ, where SΔ is the area of the triangle formed by the centre and the chords endpoints.
